message:
fix(node): correct push_bounded ordering across 59 modules — push first, then drain overflow

Fix an off-by-one in the push_bounded() helper pattern duplicated across
59 modules in the connector, control_plane, policy, remote, repair,
replay, runtime, and security subsystems.

The old pattern:
  if items.len() >= cap { drain overflow }
  items.push(item);

This checked length before pushing, meaning the drain calculation
(items.len() + 1 - cap) was speculative and the push always succeeded —
even when the vector was already at capacity, the item was pushed without
any prior drain if len was exactly cap-1.

The corrected pattern:
  items.push(item);
  if items.len() > cap { drain overflow }

By pushing first and then draining, the overflow calculation is based
on the actual post-push length, eliminating the off-by-one window. This
matches the canonical push_bounded implementation used in the engine
crate.

All 59 files contain the same mechanical fix to their local
push_bounded() helper function. No logic changes beyond the reordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
